tasks.title: Optimize Vite build chunking to resolve large chunk warnings
evidence: Vite build warning: Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification
Recommendation from Vite: Use dynamic import() to code-split, or build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks
plan: Analyze current chunk sizes and identify large dependencies causing warnings
Implement dynamic imports for routes or heavy components to enable code splitting
Configure manualChunks in vite.config.ts to group vendor dependencies logically
Verify build warnings are resolved and measure impact on initial load time
Run make ci to ensure no regressions
tasks.request:
(!) Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification. Consider:
- Using dynamic import() to code-split the application
- Use build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks to improve chunking: https://rollupjs.org/configuration-options/#output-manualchunks
- Adjust chunk size limit for this warning via build.chunkSizeWarningLimit.

tasks.title: Create UI + CLI editors for render profiles and pipeline JS
evidence: Render profiles and pipeline JS are configured via JSON files and CLI only supports list (internal/cli/manage/render_profiles.go, internal/cli/manage/pipeline_js_mgmt.go).
docs/usage.md instructs manual edits to DATA_DIR/render_profiles.json and pipeline_js.json; Web UI lacks editors.
plan: Add CRUD CLI subcommands for render-profiles and pipeline-js (add/update/delete) with validation.
Build Web UI editors with form validation and JSON preview for each config type.
Wire editors into App.tsx with clear sections and empty-state guidance.
Add tests for CLI parsing/validation and UI form validation.
Update docs/usage.md with new commands and UI workflows.
Verify by running `make test-ci` and manually creating a profile in `make web-dev`.
notes: Why it matters: render profiles and per-target JS are core to scraping reliability; editing JSON by hand is error-prone.
Proposed approach: MVS provides form-based editors + CLI CRUD; expand to per-host testing and profile import/export.
Risks/mitigations: invalid config breaks scraping; mitigate with schema validation and dry-run tests.
Acceptance criteria: CRUD works via CLI + UI; validation blocks bad configs; tests cover both; docs updated.

tasks.title: Add OAuth2 CLI support for auth profiles
evidence: OAuth endpoints are implemented in API (internal/api/auth.go) and Web UI exposes OAuth config (web/src/components/AuthConfig.tsx).
CLI has no OAuth subcommands (rg internal/cli shows no oauth references).
plan: Add `spartan auth oauth` subcommands: discover, initiate, refresh, revoke, and token-status.
For initiate, print authorization URL and instructions; optional local callback helper for token capture.
Store/retrieve tokens via existing OAuth store in internal/auth.
Add CLI tests covering expected outputs and error paths.
Update docs/usage.md with OAuth CLI examples and security notes.
Verify by running `go test ./internal/cli/...` and a manual OAuth flow against a test provider.
notes: Why it matters: OAuth-protected targets are common; CLI parity enables automation and headless use.
Proposed approach: MVS adds non-interactive initiate/refresh/revoke; expand to local callback capture and PKCE helpers.
Risks/mitigations: secrets exposure; mitigate by redacting tokens in logs and requiring explicit output flags.
Acceptance criteria: OAuth CLI flows work; tests cover failures; docs updated.

tasks.title: Refresh docs for hidden CLI + API surfaces (watch/feed/batch/chains/retention/GraphQL/WS)
evidence: docs/usage.md lists primary CLI commands but omits detailed sections for watch, feed, batch, chains, retention, export-schedule, and GraphQL/WS endpoints.
CLI help includes these subcommands (internal/cli/help.go) and API exposes /graphql and /v1/ws (internal/api/server.go).
plan: Audit CLI help and OpenAPI for missing docs sections.
Add concise usage sections for watch/feed/batch/chains/retention/export-schedule.
Document GraphQL and WebSocket endpoints with minimal examples.
Add a short capability matrix linking CLI/API/Web UI features.
Verify by running `rg 'watch|feed|batch|chains|retention' docs/usage.md` and ensuring coverage.
notes: Why it matters: hidden capabilities reduce adoption and create support churn; docs are the fastest path to unlock value.
Proposed approach: MVS adds missing sections and examples; expand to dedicated docs pages per feature.
Risks/mitigations: doc drift; mitigate with references to CLI help and OpenAPI as source of truth.
Acceptance criteria: docs cover all listed capabilities with examples; no contradictions with CLI help.
